description: Fix the chat page regression where sidecar and gateway are healthy but the main chat surface stays disconnected by moving transport ownership out of ad-hoc embedded consumers into a single app-level gateway connection manager with explicit readiness states.

userStories.title: Add a shared desktop gateway connection manager
userStories.description: As the desktop shell, I need one app-level gateway connection manager so that websocket connect/auth state is owned outside individual React components.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a reusable gateway manager module that owns websocket connect, auth handshake, request dispatch, and reconnect state
Gateway manager exposes explicit transport state for disconnected, connecting, connected, and error
Gateway manager supports request/response calls without each consumer opening its own WebSocket
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Move CronTaskResultSync onto the shared gateway manager
userStories.description: As the desktop app, I want cron result sync to use the shared gateway manager so that cron background sync no longer creates its own websocket lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: CronTaskResultSync stops constructing WebSocket directly
CronTaskResultSync fetches cron data through the shared gateway manager request API
No ad-hoc gateway connect/auth handshake logic remains inside CronTaskResultSync
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Define explicit chat transport readiness state in the shell
userStories.description: As the desktop shell, I need to separate runtime health, gateway transport, chat session readiness, and render readiness so that the UI no longer treats them as one mixed 'connected' flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Add explicit chat transport readiness state separate from runtime health and render readiness
Connection card logic reads gateway transport state instead of relying on embedded app.connected alone
Chat lifecycle helpers distinguish gateway-connected from chat-session-ready
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire OpenClawChatSurface to the shared gateway transport state
userStories.description: As a user, I want the main chat surface to reflect real gateway readiness so that the chat page stops showing 'connecting' while the gateway is already healthy.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenClawChatSurface consumes shared gateway transport state from the shell-level manager
Chat connection card only shows when gateway transport is genuinely unavailable or auth fails
When gateway transport is connected and chat session bootstraps successfully, the chat page enters a stable ready state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove embedded-transport assumptions from chat reconnect logic
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I need reconnect logic to stop assuming the embedded openclaw-app owns transport truth so that reconnect behavior does not flap when the embedded surface remounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat reconnect scheduling no longer treats embedded app.connected as the only transport source of truth
Embedded chat resets do not drop the shell-level gateway transport state
Transport recovery and render recovery remain distinct code paths
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add regression coverage for healthy sidecar but disconnected chat UI
userStories.description: As a release owner, I need regression coverage for the case where sidecar health is live but the chat page incorrectly stays disconnected so that this class of bug cannot silently return.
acceptanceCriteria: Add automated coverage for sidecar healthy plus gateway transport connected yielding a non-connecting chat surface state
Add automated coverage that cron background sync does not require its own direct WebSocket constructor
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
